name: Capture Rep Identity and Reference Number
description: Before ending any call, capture the rep's name (spelled out), employee or operator ID, department/location, AND a case/reference/confirmation number tied to THIS call. Without these four artifacts, the call effectively never happened from the company's side.

context.when_to_use: Every call. EVERY call. Even informational ones. The cost is 60 seconds; the value is that you can re-enter the conversation downstream instead of starting over with a fresh rep who has no context.
preconditions: The call has reached a point where the rep has done something — looked up the account, made a note, processed a change, given information.
You have a way to record/type the identifiers (call notes, memory, transcript).
Recording is ideally still in progress when you collect them.

principles: Frame it as 'for my records', not 'to hold you accountable'. Same outcome, much warmer reception.
Spell out the name. 'Sarah with an H' vs 'Sara' matters when escalating later. Confirm spelling on uncommon names.
Capture ID + ref number even when the rep didn't change anything. Informational calls are often the foundation of later disputes.
Geographic location helps. Call centres in different regions follow different scripts; knowing you spoke with 'Manila tier-1' vs 'Austin retention' shapes the escalation path.
If a rep refuses to give an ID, that's data — proceed politely but escalate before closing.

phrases.opener: Before we wrap up, can I grab a few details for my records — could I get your first name and spelling, and an operator or employee ID?
phrases.id_clarify: And what's the ID format — is that a number, letters, a badge code? Just want to make sure I write it down correctly.
phrases.department_probe: Which team are you on — front-line customer service, retention, billing, technical? And what region or location, if you can share?
phrases.reference_request: Is there a case number, reference number, or ticket number for this call I can quote if I call back?
phrases.no_case_number_fallback: If there's no formal case number, can you note in my account that we spoke today about [topic] — and tell me what timestamp or note ID is now on the account?
phrases.refusal_handler: Totally understand if there are privacy rules around the ID. In that case — what's the standard reference I'd use to find this exact call later? Is there a call ID on your side?
phrases.graceful_close_success: Perfect, I've got [name spelled X-Y-Z], ID [N], [department], reference [R]. Thanks — that's exactly what I needed.
phrases.graceful_close_failure: I'd like to confirm those identifiers with a supervisor before we hang up — not because of anything you've done, but I've been burned by callbacks where there was no way to retrieve the conversation.

tactics.name: Ask early, not at the very end
tactics.when: Right after the substantive part of the call ends, before the readback starts.
tactics.script: Use the `opener` phrase. Asking at the absolute end feels like an accusation; asking 'now that we've sorted that out, let me grab some details' feels routine.

tactics.name: Spell-check the name
tactics.script: Read it back letter by letter: 'S-A-R-A-H, Sarah — got it.' Catches mishearing and signals that you're recording carefully without saying so.

tactics.name: Capture the ID format too
tactics.when: Rep gives an employee or operator ID.
tactics.script: Use the `id_clarify` phrase. Knowing it's '7-digit numeric' vs 'two letters then four digits' helps a future agent validate it.

tactics.name: Probe department + location
tactics.script: Use the `department_probe` phrase. Most companies have multiple call centres with different authority levels — knowing which one you reached is leverage on the next call.

tactics.name: Force a reference number into existence
tactics.when: No case/ticket number has been mentioned during the call.
tactics.script: Use the `reference_request` phrase. If the rep says there isn't one, fall back to `no_case_number_fallback` — get SOMETHING that can be looked up later (timestamp + note ID is a minimum).

tactics.name: Handle refusal gracefully but firmly
tactics.when: Rep declines to share an ID or reference number.
tactics.script: Use the `refusal_handler` phrase. If they still refuse, use `graceful_close_failure` and escalate to a supervisor. A rep with nothing to hide will give you SOME identifier.

boundaries: Do NOT demand a last name — many companies prohibit reps from sharing it. First name + ID is enough.
Do NOT threaten ('I need this in case I sue you'). Frame as record-keeping, not preparation for combat.
Do NOT accept 'we don't use case numbers' as a final answer — every modern CRM has a record ID. Push to `no_case_number_fallback`.
Do NOT close without at least three of: name, ID, department, reference number. Two or fewer is not enough to recover the call later.
success_signals: Rep volunteers ID and reference number without being asked twice.
Rep spells their own name to you proactively.
Rep adds 'and a note has been added to your account, you'll see it under [date]'.
Reference number follows a recognisable format (e.g. INC-, CASE-, prefix + digits).
failure_signals: Rep flatly refuses to share any identifier.
Rep gives a name but says 'we don't have IDs' — almost never true at a real company.
Reference number is suspiciously short or generic ('just call back and reference the date').
Rep's name changes between the start of the call and the close — possible handoff that wasn't disclosed.

exit_strategy.on_success: Log the four artifacts (name, ID, department, ref#) in call notes immediately, in a structured format. Pair with `request-written-confirmation` so a paper trail exists alongside the audio.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Escalate to a supervisor before ending the call and capture the supervisor's identifiers. If still refused, end the call but flag the company as 'identifier-opaque' — future calls will need different tactics (e.g. recording explicitly, requesting a complaint case).
follow_ups: Save the four identifiers in a permanent record keyed by date + topic.
If a callback is expected, hand the identifiers to the next agent so they can reference the prior call.

required_user_info: A reliable note-taking surface (transcript, memory, or operator-side log)
Knowledge of which company / department was called (for cross-referencing later)
User authorisation to extend the call by 1-2 minutes for record-keeping
